texto: My heart always timidly hides itself behind my mind. I set out to bring down stars from the sky, then, for fear of ridicule, I stop and pick little flowers of eloquence.

texto: The dream, alone, is of interest. What is life, without a dream?

texto: A kiss, when all is told, what is it? An oath taken a little closer, a promise more exact. A wish that longs to be confirmed, a rosy circle drawn around the verb 'to love'. A kiss is a secret which takes the lips for the ear, a moment of infinity humming like a bee, a communion tasting of flowers, a way of breathing in a little of the heart and tasting a little of the soul with the edge of the lips!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edmond Rostand: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “A kiss, when all is told, what is it? An oath taken a little closer, a promise more exact. A wish…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, kiss, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: A kiss! When all is said, what is a kiss? An oath of allegiance taken in closer proximity, a promise more precise, a seal on a confession, a rose-red dot upon the letter i in loving; a secret which elects the mouth for ear; an instant of eternity murmuring like a bee; balmy communion with a flavor of flowers; a fashion of inhaling each other's hearts, and of tasting, on the brink of the lips, each other's soul!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edmond Rostand: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “A kiss! When all is said, what is a kiss? texto: I carry my adornments on my soul.I do not dress up like a popinjay;But inwardly, I keep my daintiness.I do not bear with me, by any chance,An insult not yet washed away- a conscienceYellow with unpurged bile- an honor frayedTo rags, a set of scruples badly worn.I go caparisoned in gems unseen,Trailing white plumes of freedom, garlandedWith my good name- no figure of a man,But a soul clothed in shining armor, hungWith deeds for decorations, twirling- thus-A bristling wit, and swinging at my sideCourage, and on the stones of this old townMaking the sharp truth ring, like golden spurs!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edmond Rostand: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “I carry my adornments on my soul.I do not dress up like a popinjay;But inwardly, I keep my dainti…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, adornments, deeds pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de deeds e honor.

texto: I have a different idea of elegance. I don't dress like a fop, it's true, but my moral grooming is impeccable. I never appear in public with a soiled conscience, a tarnished honor, threadbare scruples, or an insult that I haven't washed away. I'm always immaculately clean, adorned with independence and frankness. I may not cut a stylish figure, but I hold my soul erect. I wear my deeds as ribbons, my wit is sharper then the finest mustache, and when I walk among men I make truths ring like spurs.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Edmond Rostand: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I have a different idea of elegance. I don't dress like a fop, it's true, but my moral grooming i…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, conscience, elegance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e elegance.

I never appear in public with a soiled conscience, a tarnished honor, threadbare scruples, or an insult that I haven't washed away. I'm always immaculately clean, adorned with independence and frankness. I may not cut a stylish figure, but I hold my soul erect. I wear my deeds as ribbons, my wit is sharper then the finest mustache, and when I walk among men I make truths ring like spurs.

texto: ...But...to sing,to dream, to smile, to walk, to be alone, be free,with a voice that stirs and an eye that still can see!To cock your hat to one side, when you pleaseat a yes, a no, to fight, or- make poetry!To work without a thought of fame or fortune,on that journey, that you dream of, to the moon!Never to write a line that's not your own...

texto: Cyrano: The leaves---Roxane: What color---Perfect Venetian red! Look at them fall.Cyrano: Yes---they know how to die. A little wayFrom the branch to the earth, a little fearOf mingling with the common dust---and yetThey go down gracefully---a fall that seemsLike flying!

texto: I know that in the end you'll overwhelm me, but I'll still fight you as long as there's a breath in my body... Yes, you've robbed me of everything: the laurels of glory, the roses of love! But there's one thing you can't take away from me. When I go to meet God this evening, and doff my hat before the lofty gates, my salute will sweep the blue threshold of heaven, because I'll still have one thing intact, without a stain, something that I'll take with me in spite of you: My white plume.
explicacao: Citação de Edmond Rostand sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I know that in the end you'll overwhelm me, but I'll still fight you as long as there's a breath …” articula coragem, honor, still em registro de coragem, com relevância para contextos como termino e honor.
